entries.text: The tracks of a demon convoy are clear to see…too clear, in fact. There’s an ambush waiting ahead. Reveal to the players what kind of force they will be contending with and suggest a way to turn the tables.

The grinding of some gargantuan machinery cuts through the howling winds. In addition to whatever else the Demons have going on, an apocryphon-scale drill is tearing through the landscape nearby, its ignosis hunting for something like a bloodhound. The demonic forces in the area are split between the point of interest and the drill itself.

The party comes across a battle in progress. Roll a D6. 1-2: Two demonic factions are fighting 3-4: The demons are fighting enraged wildlife or, depending on the state of the current campaign, friendly partisans. 5-6: The demons are fighting themselves. Inner conflicts have spilled over into open warfare. Intervening on either side might be rewarding, or they could immediately turn on the party. It's Hell, after all.

The party discovers the edges of a huge scar, leading them to what looks like an enormous shattered crystal snowflake. The corpse of a nameless Archon rests within, tangled and halfformed. An escapee from the war? A traitor on their way to alert Hell? You'll never know now. Its body has drained out, golden ichor in mercury-like pools waiting to be collected. Perhaps someone in DIS can make use of this?

A procession is approaching from another direction, but not a hostile one. Eyeless giants bearing immense gold palanquins trudge, without word, along a depressed ravine in the soil they themselves stomped out over centuries of unceasing march. Most of the platforms are empty, their skeletal riders holding wisps of reins or dangling by threadbare silks from the seats. One near the back has been converted into a cargo hauler for a makeshift merchant caravan, the giants slowly making their way between several "unholy" sites operating on instructions long forgotten. The merchants are hesitant to speak, but are not meaningfully armed. If the party seems willing to trade, they can be convinced to give some tidbits of info on the region, perhaps revealing a location or the local Boss' recent endeavor.

A camp lies on the horizon, the inhabitants armed with nought but broken, rusted weapons and magics that spark more than they scorch. A dozen banners of armies long dead or never existed fly from their shabby tents. They are on guard, but a terrible sense of resignation reigns over the soldiers. They are the Faithless, a mercenary company who are damned to wander from master to master, every cause they attach themselves to giving them another defeat but continued existence. They cannot die, they cannot rest, yet they cannot win. They will reject almost any offer of cooperation or charity. They might offer information, and some of it might be useful. Perhaps they hold a cherished relic of a better day. It won't take much to get them to part with it; what good would it do them?

An enormous ritual altar lies ahead. On closer inspection, things look out of place. The incongruent markings of several different traditions sprawl in gibberish lines, sealing circles lie broken or incomplete, and the priest guiding the ritual is merely a lesser demon using a flimsy glamor to pretend to be a Prince thought long dead. It won't take long to figure out that they are attempting—attempting is the key word here—a ritual of great power. All they know, however, is what they've pieced together from ancient tomes and mere observation from a distance. Helping them is probably a bad idea.

The party discovers an incongruity up ahead. A replica of a small town on Earth from another century sits in a little valley. The inhabitants claim to be human but are very much not, and they will fight to the death if you attempt to seize anything from the town. Feel free to put on a charming regional accent when speaking for the demons in this area. Shortly after arriving, hostile demons begin to attack the town from the opposite direction.

An enemy convoy has stalled out up ahead. A landslide has blocked the path, and various menials are focusing on clearing the path ahead. You might allow them to finish before striking, and risk some of the loot escaping, or strike now, and find yourself in the exact same canyon, potentially to be ambushed from the rear just like the convoy.

A convoy bearing unknown sigils has arrived ahead of you. Whoever this is must be important: their entourage is being welcomed as honored guests, their flags are being raised above the location, and their minions are co-mingling. Something gargantuan wrapped in a bound, red tarp is being unloaded from a vehicle ahead. The next conflict will likely involve several factions; surely there’s a way to manipulate this to your advantage.

The Corpse-Engine of a Hell Titan is plainly visible, lashed and constrained behind the next point of interest. These monsters were weapons of cosmic destruction in long past eras, when man still cloaked themselves in gold. How this faction came to possess one, how they’re made, how many of them are left and who has them are all good questions to ask while exploring the next location; it’s all but certain a faction boss will end up activating the Corpse-Engine at some point during the assault.

The land here is giving way. The souls of the soil have felt the presence of their brethren and rebel against their eternal jailors. The point of interest will be devoured entirely by the Hellish wastes soon, which will certainly get in the way of your exploration. Be ready for rooms to collapse, exits to vanish, and your surroundings to collapse into the boundless catacombs deep in the vengeful chthon.

A beam of heavenly light parts the sky above your next location, like a beacon visible from hundreds of miles around. The rotten stench of marble and gold and the shrill soulless horking of trumpets close in the closer you approach. Heaven is empty, the angelic hosts are dead… so what is happening here?

This place is ruined. It’s been abandoned and hollowed for decades at least. Shriveled skeletal forms little the hallways. Malfunctioning tech and rotten demonic fixtures draw the HELLPIERCERS deeper and deeper into the dark, empty facility. For all TACCOM scenes in this facility, cap all enemy HP to 1, representing the skeletal and spectral nature of the foes. HELLPIERCERS may uncover a strange ritual experiment, an angelic corpse with bones of black stone or a pocketed hylic dimension locked in stasis. After they uncover this or some other baffling truth, time begins to flow in reverse in this cursed location, with the failure of the experiment rippling back inwardly, reviving the demonic legions inside. Restore enemy HP to their defaults during following TACCOM scenes.

Syzygos’s light shines a haunting red glow over the scene below you. Demons at this location have reverted dozens of souls back to human form, given them weapons, and forced them to fight in a bloodpit. Their immortal form still suffers—worse, even, in the sisters' red light. The garrison is completely distracted, though.

The demons have suspended a device above the location. Any HELLPIERCER can immediately identify it as an AN602 thermonuclear atomic bomb, with a yield of around 58 megatons. An explosion half that size still scars the gateways of the divine congress in Heaven.

A thick cloud of gas envelopes the area. It hangs low to the ground and looking closely at the reddish-purple hues reveals a kaleidoscopic miasma of exploding fractals and endless swirls of shapes and colors. This is gaseous Hyper-LSD, or at least a derivative of it. Tread carefully.

Any HELLPIERCER with Truth-Seeking Eyes can see a different angle to this location. If one looks katawardly, another faction’s building has overlaid itself with this one across a seventh spatial dimension. It shouldn’t be too hard to navigate both at the same time in a practical sense, but it makes any objective here much more complicated. Select a building from a faction not in active play. At any point, players may move between these buildings by walking in a kataward direction. Taking a dosage of Hyper-LSD or descending into a ritual trance also does the trick.

As you begin your approach, the sky darkens. Syzygos turns to face you, her cracked face leaking a strange ethereal white light that spreads out from the shattered and ruined planetoid like a spider's web. Lashes of purple plasma arc around the blackened stone. Within a second or two, all light seems to have vanished from the sky but for the strange halo crowning the fallen sister. It is pitch black now; you can hear the screams of the demonic forces, panicking somewhere within the complex.

The location you seek bursts forth from the horizon. A gargantuan set of tank treads, three enormous rocket engines, and a strange sail-like protrusion propel it at dizzying speeds across the land. It shows no signs of slowing down.
